questions.q: In which field is Raj Reddy a recognized pioneer?
questions.explanation: Raj Reddy is a pioneering computer scientist known for his foundational work in artificial intelligence.

questions.q: Which prestigious award, often called the 'Nobel Prize of Computing', did Raj Reddy receive?
questions.explanation: Raj Reddy won the ACM Turing Award, computing's most prestigious honor, in 1994.

questions.q: Raj Reddy is best known for pioneering which technology that lets computers understand the spoken word?
questions.explanation: Reddy was a pioneer of large-scale, continuous speech recognition systems.

questions.q: In which country was Raj Reddy born?
questions.explanation: Raj Reddy was born in India, in the village of Katur in present-day Andhra Pradesh.

questions.q: At which U.S. university did Raj Reddy spend most of his career as a professor of computer science?
questions.explanation: Raj Reddy has been a longtime faculty member at Carnegie Mellon University in Pittsburgh.

questions.q: Raj Reddy was one of the first people of which heritage to win the Turing Award?
questions.explanation: Reddy was the first person of Asian origin, and of Indian descent, to receive the Turing Award.

questions.q: The systems Raj Reddy worked on aimed to let humans interact with computers using what?
questions.explanation: His research focused on enabling computers to understand and respond to natural spoken language.

questions.q: Which of these best describes Raj Reddy's profession?
questions.explanation: Raj Reddy is a computer scientist specializing in artificial intelligence and human-computer interaction.

questions.q: Raj Reddy's pioneering work helped lay the foundations for which everyday technology used in voice assistants?
questions.explanation: His early speech recognition research underpins the voice technologies used in modern digital assistants.

questions.q: What is Raj Reddy's nickname or subtitle in the context of his contributions to computing?
questions.explanation: Raj Reddy is celebrated as a pioneer of artificial intelligence.

questions.q: In what year did Raj Reddy receive the Turing Award?
questions.explanation: Raj Reddy received the ACM Turing Award in 1994, sharing it with Edward Feigenbaum.

questions.q: Raj Reddy shared his 1994 Turing Award with which other AI researcher?
questions.explanation: The 1994 Turing Award was jointly given to Raj Reddy and Edward Feigenbaum for their work in large-scale AI systems.

questions.q: For what broad contribution were Reddy and Feigenbaum cited in their Turing Award?
options: Pioneering the design of large-scale artificial intelligence systems
questions.explanation: Their Turing Award recognized pioneering work on the design and construction of large-scale AI systems demonstrating its practical importance.

questions.q: Which famous early speech-understanding system at Carnegie Mellon is associated with Raj Reddy's group?
questions.explanation: Reddy's group developed the Hearsay-II speech-understanding system, a landmark in AI research.

questions.q: Before joining Carnegie Mellon, Raj Reddy was a faculty member at which California university?
questions.explanation: Reddy was on the faculty at Stanford University before moving to Carnegie Mellon in 1969.

questions.q: Raj Reddy was a founding director of which influential institute at Carnegie Mellon?
questions.explanation: Reddy was the founding director of the Robotics Institute at Carnegie Mellon University in 1979.

questions.q: Where did Raj Reddy earn his Ph.D. in computer science?
questions.explanation: Reddy earned his Ph.D. in computer science from Stanford University in 1966.

questions.q: Raj Reddy served as dean of which school at Carnegie Mellon during the 1990s?
questions.explanation: Reddy served as dean of Carnegie Mellon's School of Computer Science from 1991 to 1999.

questions.q: Which major U.S. science honor did Raj Reddy receive in 2006 for lifetime contributions and statesmanship in science?
questions.explanation: In 2006 Reddy received the Vannevar Bush Award from the U.S. National Science Board for lifetime contributions to science and long-standing statesmanship on its behalf.

questions.q: Which Indian civilian honor was conferred on Raj Reddy by the Government of India in 2001?
questions.explanation: Raj Reddy was awarded the Padma Bhushan, one of India's highest civilian honors, in 2001.

questions.q: Raj Reddy co-founded an Indian university aimed at engineering and IT education in Hyderabad. Which is it?
options: International Institute of Information Technology, Hyderabad (IIIT-H)
questions.explanation: Reddy played a key role in founding the International Institute of Information Technology, Hyderabad (IIIT-H).

questions.q: Raj Reddy was a co-chair of which U.S. President's advisory committee on information technology in the late 1990s?
questions.explanation: Reddy co-chaired the President's Information Technology Advisory Committee (PITAC) under President Bill Clinton.

questions.q: Which DARPA-funded program of the 1970s, focused on understanding connected speech, was a major focus of Reddy's research?
options: The ARPA Speech Understanding Research (SUR) program
questions.explanation: Reddy led work under DARPA's Speech Understanding Research (SUR) program, producing systems like Harpy and Hearsay-II.

questions.q: The Harpy speech recognition system from Reddy's lab is notable for introducing which search technique?
questions.explanation: Harpy popularized beam search, an efficient heuristic for navigating large speech-recognition search spaces.

questions.q: The Hearsay-II system introduced an influential AI software architecture known by what name?
questions.explanation: Hearsay-II pioneered the blackboard architecture, where independent knowledge sources cooperate via a shared data structure.

questions.q: In which Indian village was Raj Reddy born in 1937?
questions.explanation: Raj Reddy was born on 13 June 1937 in the village of Katur in the Madras Presidency, now in Andhra Pradesh.

questions.q: Reddy championed the idea of making computing accessible to non-literate users, an initiative he promoted under what broad theme?
options: Information and communication technologies for development (the 'PCtvt' / digital divide work)
questions.explanation: Reddy long advocated bridging the digital divide, including projects like a low-cost networked PC (PCtvt) for rural and non-literate communities.

questions.q: From which university did Raj Reddy receive his master's degree before his doctorate?
questions.explanation: Reddy earned a master's degree from the University of New South Wales in Australia before his Ph.D. at Stanford.

questions.q: Reddy was made an Officer of the French Legion of Honour in which decade?
questions.explanation: Raj Reddy was made an Officer of the French Legion of Honour in 1984, awarded by French President Francois Mitterrand.

questions.q: Reddy and Feigenbaum's Turing citation emphasized AI systems built around which approach to capturing human expertise?
questions.explanation: Their award highlighted large-scale knowledge-based AI systems that encode domain expertise to solve real-world problems.
